idea.hook: Why Real Launch Footage Feels So Tense
facts: A launch sequence is a timed handoff between ground systems, engines, guidance software, and range safety before liftoff.
Countdown calls, engine events, and holds are real mission steps, not added drama.
idea.payoff: A launch sequence is a timed handoff between ground systems, engines, guidance software, and range safety before liftoff.
idea.title: Why Real Launch Footage Feels So Tense

This is real launch-system footage. The tension comes from countdowns, engine hardware, and mission audio building before liftoff.

A launch sequence is a timed handoff between ground systems, engines, guidance software, and range safety before liftoff.

Source clip: Artemis I Launch from NASA Causeway (Slo Mo)

leader_kit.pinnedComment: A launch sequence is a timed handoff between ground systems, engines, guidance software, and range safety before liftoff. What should this lesson explain next?
replyTemplates: The calls you hear in launch footage usually mark real checks across ground systems, vehicle systems, and mission control.
The tension is real because everything is being sequenced before the rocket is allowed to leave the pad.
leader_kit.disclosure: Uses NASA source media and public information. Do not imply NASA endorsed the finished video or AI output.

posting_decision.reason: Performance and timing both support an extra post today.
